hero.title: Welcome to our spiritual home in Cologne
hero.subtitle: A place of worship, fellowship, and the living Word of God.
hero.quote: "A little one shall become a thousand, and a small one a strong nation: I the LORD will hasten it in his time." (Isaiah 60:22)

journey.title: Our Journey of Faith
journey.text: What began as a small prayer gathering in 1995 has blossomed into a vibrant, God-led community. Explore our history and see how the Lord transformed a humble beginning into a lasting spiritual sanctuary in Cologne.

timeline.startDescription: A small group met for prayer, laying the foundation of our community.
timeline.presentDescription: A spiritual sanctuary serving the heart of Cologne.

foundation.title: Our Foundation
foundation.text: Our faith is deeply rooted in the Holy Scriptures. We stand firm in the truth of the Gospel and the mystery of the Triune God, committed to living out our calling in Christ.

list.title: Youth Ministry
list.text: Empowering a "Chosen Generation" to discover their purpose and identity as citizens of Heaven.

list.title: Children’s Ministry
list.text: Following Proverbs 22:6, we nurture our children’s spiritual growth to build a firm foundation for their future.

donationSection.note: Giving is voluntary and given with love.

humbleBeginning.title:
A Humble 
 Beginning
humbleBeginning.text: The spiritual journey of the Cologne Church began on April 16, 1995. Our beginning was humble, as we initially gathered in the homes of a few believers. Despite these small beginnings, our hunger for the Word of God was immense. This period of prayer and fellowship in homes laid the foundation for our growth. As God opened more doors, we transitioned to meeting in smaller halls.

evolution.title: The Evolution
evolution.subtitle: From a Migrant Church to a Settled Community
evolution.text:
In the early years of our history, our church primarily served as a spiritual haven for students and migrants who had come to Germany for studies or temporary stays. Many members would return to their home countries after completing their education, yet the church persisted in faithfully sowing the Word of God. 
 
 Through God's grace, this dynamic has evolved over time. Today, our church has transformed into a stable and deep-rooted community of permanent residents. After 21 years of faithful fellowship, this stability led to our formal registration as Äthiopische Evangelische Gemeinde Köln Mühlheim e.V. at the District Court of Cologne (Amtsgericht Köln) under the registration number VR 19124 on November 24, 2016. This transition has provided the church with greater stability, expanded our ministries, and allowed us to build a strong foundation for the coming generation.

perseverance.title: Perseverance Through Trials
perseverance.text: The history of our church has not been without its challenges. Over the years, our journey has been marked by various obstacles and difficult circumstances. However, these trials served as tools to refine our faith and allow us to experience God’s faithfulness firsthand. Because the Lord has sustained us with His grace through every hurdle, our church stands today on a firm foundation.

ministries.title: Our Ministries Today
ministries.description: Today, the Cologne Church is active in various areas of ministry to strengthen the spiritual life of its members

worship.title: Worship Ministry
worship.text: Worship in spirit and truth that draws the hearts of believers closer to the Lord.

bibleStudy.title: In-depth Bible Study
bibleStudy.text: Programs designed to help believers explore the Word of God and be deeply rooted in divine truth.

quote.text: A little one shall become a thousand, and a small one a strong nation. I, the Lord, will hasten it in its time.

statemenList.title: Concerning the Holy Scriptures
statemenList.text: We believe that the Bible (Old and New Testaments), consisting of 66 books, is the divinely inspired and immutable Word of God. "Sola Scriptura " (Scripture Alone) is our sole and sovereign authority for faith, life, and church order. (2 Tim. 3:16-17)

statemenList.title: Concerning the Trinity
statemenList.text: We believe in one God, known through His eternal, infinite nature and as the Creator of all. This one God is revealed in three persons (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit), who are co-equal and co-eternal in nature, existence, and authority. While God is Triune, the three persons exist in unity (Coinherence) without confusion or division. We believe this divine mystery is the way God reveals Himself to believers and makes them partakers of His life. (Matt. 28:19-20)

statemenList.title: Concerning the Lord Jesus Christ
statemenList.text: We believe that the Lord Jesus Christ is the Son of God by nature, fully God and fully man. We believe He was born of the Virgin Mary through the overshadowing of the Holy Spirit; died on the cross for our sins; was buried; descended into Hades (the place of the dead) and conquered the power of death; and rose bodily on the third day. We believe He ascended into heaven, sits at the right hand of the Father, intercedes for us today, and will return in great glory. (Col. 1:19-20)

statemenList.title: Concerning the Holy Spirit
statemenList.text: Just as the Lord Jesus Christ came forth from the Father (John 8:42, 16:28), the Holy Spirit also proceeds from the Father (John 15:26) and is the third person of the Trinity with His own distinct agency. We believe the Holy Spirit is God, to be worshiped and glorified with the Father and the Son. He is a personal Spirit who sanctifies, guides, and comforts believers, and convicts the world of sin. (John 15:26, 16:12-14)

statemenList.title: Concerning the Fall and Human Sinfulness
statemenList.text: We believe that although man was created in the image and likeness of God, he fell through transgression, and as a result, sin entered the entire human race. Consequently, all humans are sinful by nature, are fallen short of the glory of God, and cannot escape the judgment of death and sin through their own efforts. Salvation is obtained only by grace (Sola Gratia) through faith in Christ Jesus. When a person repents and accepts the Lord, they are born again and receive the authority to be children of God. (Eph. 2:8, John 1:12-13)

statemenList.title: The Fullness and Gifts of the Holy Spirit
statemenList.text: We believe that a person saved by faith in Jesus Christ is sealed and filled with the Holy Spirit and receives the gifts of the Spirit (Eph. 1:13, Acts 2:4, 1 Cor. 12:7-11). We believe the Holy Spirit has a dual aspect of filling in a believer’s life

list.label: Inward Filling
list.detail: For the believer’s spiritual growth and sanctification.

list.label: Outward Filling
list.detail: Clothing the believer with spiritual power for service and gospel witness. We believe the various spiritual gifts given for the edification of the Church are still active today. (1 Cor. 12:7-11)

statemenList.title: Concerning the Church
statemenList.text: The Church is the assembly of believers redeemed by the blood of Christ, and Christ Himself is its foundation (Matt. 16:18). We believe it is the Body of Christ and the dwelling place of the Holy Spirit. All believers are one in spirit and must practically manifest this universal unity through the life of the local church. (Matt. 16:18, Eph. 2:18, 4:4)

statemenList.title: Church Ordinances
statemenList.text: While the church performs various rites, the following three are commanded and established by the Lord Jesus Christ

list.label: Water Baptism
list.detail: A mandate given to the Church before His ascension, performed in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. (Matt. 28:19-20)

list.label: The Lord’s Supper
list.detail: Established by Christ’s command as a divine ordinance to remember His death and resurrection. Believers must partake after self-examination. (Luke 22:14-20, 1 Cor. 11:23-34)

list.label: Holy Matrimony
list.detail: Christian marriage is an institution established by God (Gen. 2:21-25). It is conducted only between a man and a woman of opposite biological sexes. Since God joins them, no man shall separate them. (Mark 10:6-9)

statemenList.title: Concerning the End Times
statemenList.text: We believe in the physical resurrection and the rapture of believers. Following the rapture, believers will stand before the Bema Seat of Christ to receive rewards according to their works. We believe Jesus Christ will return to earth with His saints to establish the Millennial Kingdom, followed by the resurrection of all the dead and the final Great White Throne Judgment, where the righteous are separated for eternal life and sinners for eternal punishment. (Matt. 24, Mark 13)

hero.text1: …that you may proclaim the mighty deeds of Him who called you
hero.text2: out of darkness into His marvelous light; you are a chosen people.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text: Our vision is to see the youth of our congregation as God-fearing and serving individuals who are fruitful in ministry and in their witness in everyday life.

mission.title: Our Goal (Our Mission)
mission.text: In order to achieve this vision, we focus on the following four main areas:

identity.title: Christian Identity
identity.text: Helping young people understand their heavenly citizenship and their dignity that they have in Christ Jesus as a “chosen people,” and strengthening them to stand firm in their faith in Jesus.

shaped.title: Being Shaped by the Word
shaped.text: Since it is necessary first to know the Word in order to proclaim the deeds and blessings of God, the youth are to be shaped through instruction in Holy Scripture.

saints.title: Communion of the Saints
saints.text: Being a family in which young people who have come out of darkness into the light support one another, grow in freedom, and practice genuine Christian love.

goodness.title: Living Out God’s Goodness
goodness.text: Equipping young people to show the love and goodness of God in word and deed at school, in the workplace, and among their friends, and to conduct themselves in the house of God according to Christian order.

hero.text1: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old
hero.text2: he will not depart from it.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text1: To help children develop a personal relationship with Lord Jesus Christ and grow spiritually by knowing the Word of God from an early age.
vision.text2: "And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men" (Luke 2:52). Our ministry goes beyond merely babysitting; we focus on teaching children to achieve holistic growth in the likeness of Christ.

foundation.title: Biblical Foundation
foundation.text: All our lessons are Christ-centered, based on the Bible, and tailored to be understood by children's minds.

development.title: Holistic Development
development.text: We encourage children to mature spiritually, intellectually, and socially.

connection.title: Language and Cultural Connection
connection.text: We help children in the diaspora adapt to the lifestyle in Germany without forgetting their own culture and language.

safety.title: Safety and Protection Guidelines
safety.label: To ensure parents can bring their children with full confidence, we uphold the following safety standards

teachers.title: Qualified Teachers
teachers.text: Our ministers are carefully selected, spiritually grounded, and receive ongoing training in childcare and pedagogy.

environment.title: Clean and Comfortable Environment
environment.text: Our classrooms are clean, child-friendly, and secured against potential hazards.

supervision.title: Supervision System
supervision.text: We handle this ministry with high responsibility. To prevent children from harming themselves or being affected by external influences, they are never left unsupervised.

communication.title: Parental Communication
communication.text: Parents participate based on a set schedule and have the right to observe the ministry or request information at any time.

fellowship.title: Children's Fellowship
fellowship.text: We offer various activities to make children eager to come to church. By singing, praying, and eating together, we lay the foundation for their future brotherhood and sisterhood.

service.title: Continuity of Service
service.text: Our ministry guides children from the time they join the church until they reach the age of water baptism. After baptism, they transition into the Youth Ministry.

questions.title: Answering Children's Questions
questions.text: In collaboration with parents, we strive to answer children's spiritual and personal questions, providing guidance for their next steps in life.

organization.title: Information according to § 5 DDG
organization.name: Äthiopische Evangelische Gemeinde Köln Mühlheim e.V.

register.title: Register Entry
list: Registered in the Register of Associations (Vereinsregister)

hero.label: Our Service
hero.description: Discover how we serve our community through dedicated ministries focused on spiritual growth, biblical foundation, and genuine Christian fellowship.

youthMinistry.title: Youth Ministry
youthMinistry.description: Our vision is to see the youth of our congregation as God-fearing and serving individuals who are fruitful in ministry and in their witness in everyday life. We focus on shaping identities in Christ through the Word and genuine Christian love.

childrenMinistry.title: Children's Ministry
childrenMinistry.description: Based on Proverbs 22:6, we help children develop a personal relationship with Lord Jesus Christ. We provide a safe, holistic environment where the next generation grows in wisdom, stature, and favor with God.

ministries.transitionQuote: Building a bridge between generations, anchored in the eternal Word of God.
